findings.title: startSegment integration tests entirely missing
findings.suggested_fix: Add tests: (1) GET /api/stream/:id/playlist?startSegment=5 returns playlist whose first EXTINF/segment URI carries index 005 (not 000), (2) startSegment=0 is equivalent to omitting the param, (3) startSegment equal to segmentCount returns 400, (4) negative startSegment returns 400, (5) non-numeric startSegment (e.g. 'foo') returns 400. These cover the Windows cross-segment seek path which is the only mechanism mpv clients use for mid-track seeking.

findings.title: buildPlaylist unit tests don't cover startSegment parameter
findings.suggested_fix: Add unit tests for buildPlaylist with startSegment > 0: verify (1) EXT-X-MEDIA-SEQUENCE header equals startSegment, (2) first segment URI starts at 'segment/005' (not '000') when startSegment=5, (3) EXTINF count = segmentCount - startSegment, (4) last segment EXTINF duration still uses track-relative duration calculation. The formula `durationSec - i * segmentSeconds` is correct but goes untested for startSegment > 0 cases.

findings.title: Token-via-query-param auth untested on segment endpoint
findings.suggested_fix: All segment endpoint tests authenticate via Authorization Bearer header, but mpv clients authenticate via ?token= query param. Add a test that sends GET /api/stream/:id/segment/000?bitrate=128&token=<jwt> with no Authorization header and verifies 200. This is the only auth path used by the actual Windows client and exercises a different code branch in the authenticate middleware.

findings.title: Token-via-query-param auth untested on playlist endpoint
findings.suggested_fix: The playlist auth tests only cover the case of a missing Authorization header (401). Add a test: GET /api/stream/:id/playlist?bitrate=128&token=<real-jwt> without any Authorization header returns 200. The ?token= fallback in authenticate middleware is explicitly documented in CLAUDE.md and is the primary auth mechanism for libmpv clients.

findings.title: initHlsCacheDir entirely untested
findings.suggested_fix: Add unit tests for initHlsCacheDir: (1) creates HLS_CACHE_DIR when missing, (2) deletes *.part files inside track subdirectories, (3) leaves non-.part files untouched, (4) handles a cache dir that contains non-directory entries (file-level entries) without throwing. The function is called at startup; a bug that deletes valid segments or crashes would cause streaming to fail on server restart.

findings.title: startHlsCacheCleanup entirely untested
findings.suggested_fix: Add unit tests for startHlsCacheCleanup using vi.useFakeTimers(): (1) directories newer than 24 h are not removed, (2) directories older than 24 h have all files deleted then the dir removed via rmdirSync, (3) non-directory entries in HLS_CACHE_DIR are skipped (stat.isDirectory() === false), (4) empty cache dir doesn't error. Use vi.setSystemTime() + vi.advanceTimersByTime(ONE_HOUR) to trigger the setInterval callback without wall-clock waiting.

findings.title: 404 when track not found not tested at integration level
findings.suggested_fix: Add integration tests for both endpoints: (1) GET /api/stream/99999/playlist?bitrate=128 when nock stubs Lidarr GET /api/v1/track/99999 to 404 → expect 404 from our API, (2) same for the segment endpoint. This verifies the Lidarr-404 → backend-404 error mapping in resolveFilePath flows through the HLS routes correctly.

findings.title: Concurrent segment requests dedup not tested at integration level
findings.suggested_fix: Add a test that sets mockState.delayMs=50, fires two simultaneous requests for the same segment via Promise.all(), then asserts mockState.callCount === 1 (single ffmpeg invocation) and both responses return 200 with identical body lengths. The unit test covers the dedup at the ensureSegment level but does not verify the inFlightSegments map works correctly under the route/HTTP layer (e.g. two requests could race between existsSync and inFlightSegments.get before the first promise resolves).

findings.title: _MediaKitBackend seekTo segment-boundary logic untested
findings.suggested_fix: Extract the pure segment-math logic (targetSegment = clampedMs ~/ segmentMs, withinSegment = Duration(milliseconds: clampedMs - targetSegment * segmentMs), currentSegment = _segmentOffset.inMilliseconds ~/ segmentMs) into a static/top-level function and unit test it directly. Test cases: pos=Duration.zero with offset=zero stays in same segment; pos=Duration(seconds:6) crosses boundary (targetSegment=1, withinSegment=zero); pos=Duration(seconds:7) crosses boundary with non-zero withinSegment; pos=Duration(seconds:5) with currentSegment=0 stays in segment. Platform channel isolation means _reopenQueueAt cannot be called in unit tests, but the math can be tested independently.

findings.title: _matchUriToQueueIndex prefix-substring false positive for similar track IDs
findings.suggested_fix: The prefix match (`_queueUris[i].toString().contains(prefix)`) where prefix is '/api/stream/1/' will also match a queue URI for track 10, 100, 1000 if track 1's prefix substring appears inside the longer ID. Example: queue contains URIs for tracks [1, 10]; mpv reports path for track 10 segment as '...stream/10/segment/000...'; _trackIdPattern extracts prefix '/api/stream/1' but wait — the regex is r'/api/stream/(\d+)/' which ends with '/'. So prefix '/api/stream/1/' will NOT match '/api/stream/10/' because '10' != '1'. Verify this with test: _matchUriToQueueIndex('/api/stream/10/segment/000') in a queue containing URIs for track 1 and track 10 should return index of track 10, not track 1. Add this as a unit test for _matchUriToQueueIndex exposed as @visibleForTesting or via a pure function extraction.

findings.title: getSegmentCachePath not directly unit tested
findings.suggested_fix: Add a minimal unit test for getSegmentCachePath: verify it returns a path under HLS_CACHE_DIR, joins the trackKey subdirectory, and formats the segment index as 3-digit zero-padded 'segmentNNN.mp3'. Currently only tested indirectly through ensureSegment/generateSegment.

findings.title: generateSegment does not delete .part file on rename failure
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'end' callback, if renameSync throws, the .part file is left on disk. Subsequent calls will find existsSync(segPath) === false, trigger generateSegment again, and the stale .part file will be overwritten — acceptable but the .part file is never cleaned. More importantly, this path is not tested. Add a unit test: mock renameSync to throw, verify the promise rejects with the rename error AND that unlinkSync is called on the .part path to clean up.

findings.title: Segment endpoint accepts non-padded numeric index (e.g. '5' vs '005')
findings.suggested_fix: The route parameter `:index` is parsed with parseInt, so both '/segment/005' and '/segment/5' produce segmentIndex=5 and would succeed. The playlist only emits zero-padded URIs but mpv or a buggy client could send non-padded requests. Verify the intended contract with a test: GET /api/stream/:id/segment/5?bitrate=128 (non-padded) and document whether it should return 200 or 400. If 200 is intended, add a test; if 400, add validation and a test.

findings.title: No test for playlist default bitrate (omitted bitrate param = 320)
findings.suggested_fix: parseBitrate(undefined) returns 320 per the source. Add a test: GET /api/stream/:id/playlist with no bitrate query param returns 200 and the playlist contains 'bitrate=320' in segment URIs. Verifies the default is applied end-to-end rather than only in the unit function.

residual_risks: The Windows cross-segment seek path (_MediaKitBackend.seekTo → _reopenQueueAt with startSegment) is exercised only by manual testing on Windows with a real libmpv. Platform channel constraints make automated test coverage of this path essentially zero.

The inFlightSegments dedup map is tested at unit level but not under real HTTP concurrency. A race between existsSync and inFlightSegments.get (both fast, but not atomic) could theoretically allow duplicate ffmpeg processes for the same segment under high load.

initHlsCacheDir runs at startup and has no tests; a regression there (e.g., accidentally deleting completed segments instead of only .part files) would silently degrade streaming performance and only surface at runtime.
The coverage:ignore-file pragma on audio_service.dart means coverage reports give a false sense of security — the 500+ lines of _MediaKitBackend are completely excluded from flutter coverage metrics.

The _segmentDuration constant in _MediaKitBackend is hardcoded to 6 seconds and must match HLS_SEGMENT_SECONDS on the backend. There is no runtime assertion or test that validates they match; a backend config change would cause incorrect position reporting on Windows.
testing_gaps: startSegment query parameter: zero integration tests for the primary Windows seeking mechanism
Token-via-query-param auth: no integration test verifies the ?token=<jwt> path (the only auth mechanism available to libmpv) works on either endpoint
initHlsCacheDir: zero unit tests for the startup cleanup function that removes stale .part files
startHlsCacheCleanup: zero unit tests for the hourly eviction timer and its edge cases (empty dir, non-directory entries, files actively being written)
_MediaKitBackend: entire class is excluded from coverage via pragma; no unit tests for seekTo segment math, _matchUriToQueueIndex prefix matching, or _updateFromPath offset calculation
404 from Lidarr propagation: no integration test for track-not-found on either HLS endpoint
Concurrent segment dedup: unit-level dedup test exists but no integration-level concurrent HTTP request test
generateSegment error cleanup: no test for the code path where renameSync fails after successful ffmpeg transcoding
Default bitrate fallback: parseBitrate(undefined)=320 has no integration-level test confirming the default reaches segment URIs in the playlist response
Non-padded segment index acceptance: no test documents or verifies the contract for '/segment/5' vs '/segment/005' requests
